raw_consumer_statement:
I let Dillard's/CITI know that I had noticed a charge on my card that was not mine. I let them know that my card had been cloned and that I had filed the correct paperwork and noticed that charge was on there. They sent me a new card and said it was resolved. I received a package in the mail dated XX/XX/year>, but did not arrive till XX/XX/year> stating they had verified the information that I made the charges. The information they provided says that my email address, that I don't have. If they had checked there information, they would know that I don't have that as an email address. And that the order came from XXXX and I don't live in XXXX. This however, is insufficient for them to prove that I didn't charge it. 

There is valid proof that the phone with the card information on it was cloned. Yes, my card number is/was out there for people to use. The proper notice and paperwork has been completed. The evidence they sent shows that addresses were changed and that I don't have an account with heydude to have prior orders, which the company alleges I have.
